hero.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop
hero.reason: In honor of Insurance Awareness Day, we're highlighting a film that showcases the importance of protecting and serving, even in unexpected places like a mall.
hero.blurb: This family-friendly comedy brings laughs and excitement as Paul Blart navigates the challenges of being a mall cop. With its lighthearted tone and entertaining action sequences, it's a great choice for a fun movie night.

released.title: Ratatouille
released.reason: Released today in 2007, this beloved Pixar film continues to delight audiences with its charming story and stunning animation.
released.blurb: This beautifully crafted film tells the story of Remy, a talented rat with a passion for cooking, and his journey to becoming a culinary master. With its witty dialogue and lovable characters, Ratatouille is a must-see for animation fans.

birthday.title: Kathryn
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Kathryn Beaumont, born June 27 is incorrect I used June 28, 1938,  1–2 sentence why this is one of her notable films.
birthday.blurb: This classic Disney film showcases Beaumont's talent as a voice actress, bringing the beloved character of Wendy Darling to life. With its timeless story and memorable characters, Kathryn is a delightful choice for families and Disney fans.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, this psychological thriller failed to impress audiences with its convoluted plot and poor acting.
awful.blurb: This cringeworthy film tries to pass off its messy storyline and lackluster performances as deep and thought-provoking, but ultimately falls flat. With its embarrassing attempts at suspense and drama, I Know Who Killed Me is a laughable example of a movie gone wrong.
